mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in non-farm payrolls signals rising labor demand, income, and consumer spending, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If monthly PAYEMS declines but S&P 500 still rises over the next 20 days in more than 50% of such instances, or rank IC between PAYEMS growth and forward return is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate of at least 25 basis points over three months signals an accommodative shift that lowers discount rates and boosts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines by at least 25 basis points over a 3-month period
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS triggers a 25bp 3-month decline, or the average excess return over the 60-day horizon is not positive at the 5% significance level.

mechanism_narrative: When both industrial production and housing starts accelerate concurrently, it confirms broad-based economic expansion that drives corporate profits higher, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth >0.5% AND HOUST month-over-month growth >2%
failure_condition: If under the joint condition S&P 500 forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances, or the average return is not significantly above zero (t-test p>0.05), the interaction hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-unemployment regime (UNRATE above 6%), a monthly decline in the unemployment rate signals a durable recovery that strongly lifts equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 6% AND UNRATE declines by at least 0.1 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: If in high-unemployment regimes with falling UNRATE, S&P 500 60-day returns do not have a positive mean exceeding the unconditional mean by at least 2% annualized, or the success rate is below 55% over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in real retail and food services sales (RSAFS) reflects robust consumer spending, which directly boosts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following week (5 trading days).
failure_condition: If weekly returns are negative in 50% or more of weeks when RSAFS is above its 3-month moving average, or the rank IC between RSAFS growth and 5-day forward return is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO > 1% drop) signals a supply-side contraction that quickly depresses market sentiment and industrial profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If after such declines S&P 500 rises in more than 40% of the next 5 trading days, or the average 5-day return is not negative at the 5% significance level, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation accelerates (CPIAUCSL month-over-month > 0.2%) while the federal funds rate remains below the inflation rate (negative real rates), extremely accommodative policy fuels risk-taking and speculation, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month > 0.2% AND FEDFUNDS < year-over-year CPI inflation rate
failure_condition: If in periods of negative real rates and rising inflation, S&P 500 20-day returns are not positive in more than 60% of instances, or the average return is not significantly above the unconditional mean (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During a recession (GDP contraction in the prior quarter), a positive GDP growth surprise (quarterly annualized rate above 1.5%) signals the recession's end and a strong recovery, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter (60 trading days).
candidate_trigger: Prior quarter GDPC1 growth negative AND current quarter GDPC1 annualized growth > 1.5%
failure_condition: If after such positive GDP surprises following negative quarters, S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 30% of cases, or the average return is lower than the unconditional 60-day mean by less than 50 basis points.
